heading: 4. Protocol Specification
sentence: It is mandatory that all implementations of the SNMP support the five PDUs: GetRequest-PDU, GetNextRequest-PDU, GetResponse-PDU, SetRequest-PDU, and Trap-PDU.
enhanced_sentence: All SNMP implementations must support the five core protocol data units (GetRequest-PDU, GetNextRequest-PDU, GetResponse-PDU, SetRequest-PDU, and Trap-PDU) to ensure basic protocol functionality. This requirement applies during message processing when protocol entities exchange SNMP messages encoded in UDP datagrams. GetRequest, GetNextRequest, and SetRequest PDUs are used for management operations, GetResponse PDUs carry operation results, and Trap PDUs report asynchronous events. Implementations must correctly parse, generate, and process these PDUs when received on standard UDP ports (161 for all except Trap-PDUs, which use port 162), enabling fundamental network management operations including MIB variable inspection, modification, and event notification.

This binding mechanism enables protocol entities to unambiguously identify which managed object is being referenced and what value it contains or should be set to, forming the basis for all SNMP data exchange operations.

heading: 4. Protocol Specification -> 4.1. Elements of Procedure -> 4.1.4. The GetResponse-PDU
sentence: In the ASN.1 language, the GetResponse-PDU is defined as: GetResponse-PDU ::= [2] IMPLICIT SEQUENCE { request-id RequestID, error-status ErrorStatus, error-index ErrorIndex, variable-bindings VarBindList }.
enhanced_sentence: The GetResponse-PDU is generated by an SNMP agent in response to received GetRequest-PDU, GetNextRequest-PDU, or SetRequest-PDU messages. This PDU uses ASN.1 encoding with type tag [2] and follows the structure: GetResponse-PDU ::= [2] IMPLICIT SEQUENCE { request-id RequestID, error-status ErrorStatus, error-index ErrorIndex, variable-bindings VarBindList }. The request-id must match the corresponding request to enable response correlation, error-status indicates processing success or failure, error-index identifies the specific variable binding causing an error (if any), and variable-bindings contain the requested management information or error indicators. Upon receipt, the protocol entity delivers the PDU contents to the SNMP application entity for further processing.

The expected outcome is asynchronous notification delivery without requiring prior request from the management station. heading: 4. Protocol Specification -> 4.1. Elements of Procedure -> 4.1.2. The GetRequest-PDU
sentence: The form of the GetRequest-PDU is: GetRequest-PDU ::= [0] IMPLICIT SEQUENCE { request-id RequestID, error-status -- always 0 ErrorStatus, error-index -- always 0 ErrorIndex, variable-bindings VarBindList }.
enhanced_sentence:
The GetRequest-PDU is generated by an SNMP manager to request the values of one or more managed objects from an SNMP agent. The PDU structure must follow the ASN.1 syntax: GetRequest-PDU ::= [0] IMPLICIT SEQUENCE { request-id RequestID, error-status -- always 0 ErrorStatus, error-index -- always 0 ErrorIndex, variable-bindings VarBindList }. 

This rule applies when an SNMP application entity initiates a read operation. The error-status and error-index fields must always be set to zero in the request, as they are reserved for the response. The request-id must be unique to correlate requests with responses. The variable-bindings list contains the object identifiers of the managed objects being queried.

Upon receiving a valid GetRequest-PDU, the agent processes the request according to the protocol specification and returns a GetResponse-PDU containing the requested values or appropriate error indications. The successful outcome is a GetResponse-PDU with error-status set to noError and the variable-bindings populated with the requested object values.

Prerequisites include: the SNMP session must be established, the manager must have valid object identifiers in the relevant MIB view, and the request must conform to the ASN.1 encoding rules.

heading: 5. Definitions
sentence: RFC1157-SNMP DEFINITIONS ::= BEGIN IMPORTS ObjectName, ObjectSyntax, NetworkAddress, IpAddress, TimeTicks FROM RFC1155-SMI; Message ::= SEQUENCE { version INTEGER { version-1(0) }, community OCTET STRING, data ANY } PDUs ::= CHOICE { get-request GetRequest-PDU, get-next-request GetNextRequest-PDU, get-response GetResponse-PDU, set-request SetRequest-PDU, trap Trap-PDU } GetRequest-PDU ::= [0] IMPLICIT PDU GetNextRequest-PDU ::= [1] IMPLICIT PDU GetResponse-PDU ::= [2] IMPLICIT PDU SetRequest-PDU ::= [3] IMPLICIT PDU PDU ::= SEQUENCE { request-id INTEGER, error-status INTEGER { noError(0), tooBig(1), noSuchName(2), badValue(3), readOnly(4), genErr(5) }, error-index INTEGER, variable-bindings VarBindList } Trap-PDU ::= [4] IMPLICIT SEQUENCE { enterprise OBJECT IDENTIFIER, agent-addr NetworkAddress, generic-trap INTEGER { coldStart(0), warmStart(1), linkDown(2), linkUp(3), authenticationFailure(4), egpNeighborLoss(5), enterpriseSpecific(6) }, specific-trap INTEGER, time-stamp TimeTicks, variable-bindings VarBindList } VarBind ::= SEQUENCE { name ObjectName, value ObjectSyntax } VarBindList ::= SEQUENCE OF VarBind END
enhanced_sentence:
This ASN.1 definition specifies the complete message structure for SNMP version 1 (SNMPv1) protocol operations. It governs how SNMP messages are formatted and encoded for transmission between managers and agents.

**Protocol Behavior**: Defines the core SNMPv1 protocol data units (PDUs) including request types (get, get-next, set), responses, and unsolicited trap notifications. The version field must be set to 0 for SNMPv1 compliance.

**Protocol Flow Application**: 
- GetRequest-PDU: Used by managers to retrieve specific variable values
- GetNextRequest-PDU: Used for table traversal and bulk data retrieval
- SetRequest-PDU: Used by managers to modify variable values
- GetResponse-PDU: Used by agents to respond to requests
- Trap-PDU: Used by agents to send asynchronous event notifications

**Expected Outcomes**:
- Properly formatted messages enable successful SNMP operations
- Error-status codes provide operation result indications (noError, tooBig, etc.)
- Variable-bindings carry the actual management data being exchanged
- Request-id ensures request-response correlation

**Prerequisites**: 
- Must import data types from RFC1155-SMI (Structure of Management Information)
- Community string provides basic authentication
- Network connectivity between SNMP entities must be established
- Managed devices must implement the SNMP agent supporting these PDU types

RFC1157-SNMP DEFINITIONS ::= BEGIN 
IMPORTS ObjectName, ObjectSyntax, NetworkAddress, IpAddress, TimeTicks FROM RFC1155-SMI; 

Message ::= SEQUENCE { 
    version INTEGER { version-1(0) }, 
    community OCTET STRING, 
    data ANY 
} 

PDUs ::= CHOICE { 
    get-request GetRequest-PDU,
    get-next-request GetNextRequest-PDU,
    get-response GetResponse-PDU,
    set-request SetRequest-PDU,
    trap Trap-PDU 
} 

GetRequest-PDU ::= [0] IMPLICIT PDU 
GetNextRequest-PDU ::= [1] IMPLICIT PDU 
GetResponse-PDU ::= [2] IMPLICIT PDU 
SetRequest-PDU ::= [3] IMPLICIT PDU 

PDU ::= SEQUENCE { 
    request-id INTEGER,
    error-status INTEGER { 
        noError(0), 
        tooBig(1), 
        noSuchName(2), 
        badValue(3), 
        readOnly(4), 
        genErr(5) 
    },
    error-index INTEGER,
    variable-bindings VarBindList 
} 

Trap-PDU ::= [4] IMPLICIT SEQUENCE { 
    enterprise OBJECT IDENTIFIER,
    agent-addr NetworkAddress,
    generic-trap INTEGER { 
        coldStart(0),
        warmStart(1),
        linkDown(2),
        linkUp(3),
        authenticationFailure(4),
        egpNeighborLoss(5),
        enterpriseSpecific(6) 
    },
    specific-trap INTEGER,
    time-stamp TimeTicks,
    variable-bindings VarBindList 
} 

VarBind ::= SEQUENCE { 
    name ObjectName,
    value ObjectSyntax 
} 

VarBindList ::= SEQUENCE OF VarBind 
END
